Given MSFT's huge rise these past couple of weeks, does this mean they have invested? Or are they still waiting?  Based on volume alone and it being average this past week, I would guess that they have not, but wanted to hear what you thoughts.

comment.body: Alma, I’ve been working my way through Natenberg the last few weeks—highlighter and pencil in hand (it’s a study, not a read)—and give a sincere “thank you” for the recommendation.  It is so well written, and the clarity and careful progression through the subject matter naturally build from one topic to the next.  I will add that one problem for the non-professional is the problem of too much choice and not being equipped to discern the right choice from the wrong choice, except for learning the hard way.  So while there is a wealth of free information and books are truly cheap for their value, there’s also a wealth of mediocre and just plain bad information also (especially on the internet, and especially on investment/trading), and this raises the problem of what books are worth reading, what internet sources are worthy of consideration.  That’s where your recommendations—even for free material or $50 books—are good as gold and equally as valuable.  I reiterate:  a sincere thank you for your book recommendations and of course for your work on the substack.
comment.raw_body: Alma, I’ve been working my way through Natenberg the last few weeks—highlighter and pencil in hand (it’s a study, not a read)—and give a sincere “thank you” for the recommendation.  It is so well written, and the clarity and careful progression through the subject matter naturally build from one topic to the next.  I will add that one problem for the non-professional is the problem of too much choice and not being equipped to discern the right choice from the wrong choice, except for learning the hard way.  So while there is a wealth of free information and books are truly cheap for their value, there’s also a wealth of mediocre and just plain bad information also (especially on the internet, and especially on investment/trading), and this raises the problem of what books are worth reading, what internet sources are worthy of consideration.  That’s where your recommendations—even for free material or $50 books—are good as gold and equally as valuable.  I reiterate:  a sincere thank you for your book recommendations and of course for your work on the substack.
